name: White Chocolate and Macadamia Nut Brownies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: the California Culinary Academy
Preparation Time: 0:50
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F. If macadamia nuts are salted, blanch in boiling water for 30 seconds, then rinse under cold running water and transfer to a baking sheet. Toast in the 325-degree oven until golden brown, let cool, then coarsely chop. If unsalted, just toast and chop.
2. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
3. Place butter, sugar, and water in a medium saucepan over low heat. Cut 6 ounces of white chocolate into large chunks and the remaining 3 ounces into small chips.
4. When butter has melted, remove from heat, add the 6-ounce white chocolate chunks, and stir until chocolate melts.
5. Beat eggs and vanilla. Stir in flour mixture until just blended. Add the 3-ounce white chocolate chips and nuts.
6. Spread batter in a greased 8- or 9-inch square baking pan. Bake 30 to 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool on a wire rack, then cut into squares.
Timesaver Tip: Brownie batter can be made ahead and frozen before baking. Line an 8- or 9-inch square pan with heavy-duty foil, spoon batter into the pan, freeze solid, remove from pan, wrap tightly, label, and date. Freeze at 0 degrees F up to 4 weeks. To bake, peel foil from batter and place in a greased pan. Bake in a preheated 375-degree oven until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean (40 to 50 minutes).
Microwave Version: Chocolate may be melted in a microwave oven. Place butter and chocolate in a small glass bowl. Microwave on 50% power for 1 minute, stir, then continue microwaving 1 to 3 minutes more. Stir until smooth. Do not overheat or chocolate will scorch around edges and won't blend smoothly. Stir in sugar and water and continue with step 5.
